conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you some questions. Let me start with:

**Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'd like to find out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. To help assess your eligibility, I need to gather some information about you and your child.

**What is your child's name and how old are they?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: My child's name is Alex, and they're 18 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Alex is 18 years old, I need to understand their current situation, as this affects eligibility.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** (For example, full-time non-advanced education such as A-levels, Scottish Highers, or approved training programs)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is still in education. They're doing their A-levels at sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's helpful to know. A-levels at sixth form college would typically count as approved education.

**When did Alex turn 18, or when will they turn 18?** (I need to know the specific date or at least the month and year, as this affects how long you can claim Child Benefit for them)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex turned 18 back in February this year.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. 

**Is Alex enrolled in full-time education (studying at least 12 hours per week of supervised study)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex is doing full-time A-levels, so definitely more than 12 hours a week of supervised study.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's good to know. Now I need to understand your living situation with Alex.

**Does Alex live with you, and are you responsible for them?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives with me at home, and I'm responsible for them.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. I also need to understand your residency status.

**Are you normally living in the UK, and do you have the right to claim Child Benefit here?** (For example, are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, or are you working in the UK?)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and I'm a UK resident, so I have the right to claim.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me just clarify one more important detail:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?** (For example, Alex's other parent or another person they might live with part of the time)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Here's why:**

Even though Alex is 18, you can continue to claim Child Benefit because:

✓ Alex is in approved full-time education (A-levels at sixth form college)  
✓ They are studying more than 12 hours per week of supervised study  
✓ Alex lives with you and you're responsible for them  
✓ You're a UK resident with the right to claim  
✓ No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex  

**Important to note:**

You can continue claiming Child Benefit for Alex until:
- **31st August after they turn 19**, OR
- **They turn 20**, OR  
- **They leave approved education or training**

Whichever comes first.

Since Alex turned 18 in February this year, you can continue claiming until at least 31st August next year (when they turn 19), provided they remain in full-time approved education.

If you haven't already claimed, you should apply as soon as possible, as you can only backdate claims for up to 3 months.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 18 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 18 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 18 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 18 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 18 years old and turned 18 in February of this year. According to UK Child Benefit rules, you can continue to claim Child Benefit for a child aged 16-19 if they are in approved education or training. Alex is enrolled in full-time A-level studies at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved full-time non-advanced education (studying more than 12 hours per week of supervised study). Alex lives with the claimant who is responsible for them. The claimant is a UK resident with the right to claim Child Benefit, and no one else is currently claiming for Alex. Child Benefit can continue until Alex turns 20, or until the 31st August following their 19th birthday (whichever comes first), provided they remain in approved education or training. Therefore, the claimant is eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 18-year-old child Alex. Although Alex is 18, they remain eligible because they are in full-time approved education (A-levels), live with the claimant who is responsible for them, the claimant is a UK resident with the right to claim, and no one else is claiming for Alex. Benefit can continue until the end of August after Alex turns 19 or until they turn 20/leave approved education, whichever comes first.
